messages.author: ⸸ᵉˣᵉᶜᵘᵗᵉᵈ ᵇʸ ᵈᵃᵉᵐᵒⁿˢ⸸
messages.text: Hey friends! I'm hoping to see MeshCore take off in the Chicagoland area, like Meshtastic has. I have a room server running on a Station G2 at ~140' AGL on the far north side of Chicago. Currently, it appears that I am the only node listed on the internet map in all of Illinois. I’d love to connect with others in my area who are exploring MeshCore! I still have a few Meshtastic nodes, but I’ve become increasingly disillusioned with the project and see MeshCore as a more robust and forward-thinking alternative. On a related note, I’m working on expanding the Chicagoland Reticulum network. If anyone is interested in Reticulum—whether you're local or not—I’d love to connect and collaborate! (edited)
messages.content_html: Hey friends! I'm hoping to see MeshCore take off in the Chicagoland area, like Meshtastic has. I have a room server running on a Station G2 at ~140' AGL on the far north side of Chicago. Currently, it appears that I am the only node listed on the internet map in all of Illinois. I’d love to connect with others in my area who are exploring MeshCore! I still have a few Meshtastic nodes, but I’ve become increasingly disillusioned with the project and see MeshCore as a more robust and forward-thinking alternative. On a related note, I’m working on expanding the Chicagoland Reticulum network. If anyone is interested in Reticulum—whether you're local or not—I’d love to connect and collaborate! (edited)

messages.author: Christopher A. Wichura
messages.text: I am in Lincoln Park on the 9th floor of a high rise. I just got in a Spec5 Trekker Delta yesterday, and I am not picking up anything. The meshcore map doesn't show much for this area, either, with very few nodes and most of them not updating in a while. But then I don't know how many people want to publish their location to the map, either... (unless they are putting up a repeater for the good of all)

messages.author: PiGuy9614
messages.text: The infrastructure certainly is still being built out, but the city is starting to get close to a functional network (at least far closer than us in the burbs!). Keep it up for a few days and let me know if you see anything. Bonus points if you can upload that data to LetsMesh.net using the standalone observer-native-dev firmware

messages.author: Christopher A. Wichura
messages.text: I don't have a spare Pi to run the letsmesh stuff on, unfortunately. I ordered a 5Dbi outdoor dipole antenna to see if that helps any. I've had the unit in my window, which faces west (so towards way more city instead of the lake) and also taken it for a small walkabout, and nada. I don't have another LoRa device to test with to make sure that the unit itself isn't a DOA, but I do see the noise floor changing (floats around -115+-5), which if the radio was fried I'd think would either be very erratic or have no deviations at all. (edited)
messages.content_html: I don't have a spare Pi to run the letsmesh stuff on, unfortunately. I ordered a 5Dbi outdoor dipole antenna to see if that helps any. The infrastructure certainly is still being built out, but the city is starting to get close to a functional network (at least far closer than us in the burbs!). Keep it up for a few days and let me know if you see anything. Bonus points if you can upload that data to LetsMesh.net using the standalone observer-native-dev firmware

messages.author: PiGuy9614
messages.text: Odds are the LoRa device is just fine, and luckily you don’t actually need a pi to do it anymore, that observer-native-dev firmware is standalone for ESP32-based nodes. Totally up to you on whether you’d like to host it or not, but I’d recommend it for stationary companion nodes, repeaters, or room servers. If you have any questions feel free to shoot them my way!
messages.content_html: Odds are the LoRa device is just fine, and luckily you don’t actually need a pi to do it anymore, that observer-native-dev firmware is standalone for ESP32-based nodes. Totally up to you on whether you’d like to host it or not, but I’d recommend it for stationary companion nodes, repeaters, or room servers. If you have any questions feel free to shoot them my way!

messages.author: Christopher A. Wichura
messages.text: My node is nRF, not ESP32.
messages.content_html: My node is nRF, not ESP32.

messages.author: Christopher A. Wichura
messages.text: I found the observer-native-uplink fork. Seems like from some of the comments I also saw that it may have a memory leak that leads to instability over time, requiring periodic reboots. But if I do decide to purchase a second unit to have as a repeater at home, I'll definitely make sure its ESP32-based for the option to use the fork.
messages.content_html: I found the observer-native-uplink fork. Seems like from some of the comments I also saw that it may have a memory leak that leads to instability over time, requiring periodic reboots. But if I do decide to purchase a second unit to have as a repeater at home, I'll definitely make sure its ESP32-based for the option to use the fork.

Totally up to you on whether you’d like to host it or not, but I’d recommend it for stationary companion nodes, repeaters, or room servers. If you have any questions feel free to shoot them my way!

messages.author: Christopher A. Wichura
messages.text: I have added a LetsMesh observer device called "CAW Observer 01". It's a Heltec V4 dev board running the native mqtt firmware fork. It has repeat turned off -- it's just a "listener" to feed into LetsMesh.
messages.content_html: I have added a LetsMesh observer device called "CAW Observer 01". It's a Heltec V4 dev board running the native mqtt firmware fork. It has repeat turned off -- it's just a "listener" to feed into LetsMesh.

messages.author: Uncle Lit
messages.text: the meshcore dev team have moved to a new discord server. details and reasons are here: https://blog.meshcore.io/2026/04/23/the-split Please create a new thread for your region in the https://discord.com/channels/1495203904898728149/1495412453150818585 for your region on the new meshcore.io discord server. https://meshcore.gg
